title: DecisionEvent
description: Server-sent event emitted on GET /v2/decisions:subscribe (Pillar 3). Each event is one JSON object on the SSE data line. Seven event types cover the full permit lifecycle; the 'type' field discriminates the union, and 'id' doubles as the SSE Last-Event-ID so clients resume cleanly after reconnect. Open question (see PROPOSALS/001-streaming-evaluate.md): should we reuse this envelope for the interim-decision case of streaming evaluate, or keep streaming evaluate on its own path? Recommendation: reuse — one DecisionEvent union across both streaming surfaces.

id.description: Monotonic per-org event id. SDKs echo this back in the Last-Event-ID header on reconnect so the server replays from the right position.

type.description: Event type discriminator. 'rate_limit_state' is an observability channel — the server emits it when a client's remaining budget crosses a threshold (default: 10% remaining) so dashboards can show back-pressure without polling.

org_id.description: Organization the event belongs to. Always present so subscribers multiplexing multiple keys can route locally.

emitted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the event was emitted by the server (not when the underlying action occurred — that's carried on the nested payload).

permit_id.description: Decision id this event relates to. Present on every type except 'rate_limit_state'.

actor_id.description: The actor that triggered the event — evaluator, verifier, revoker, escalator. Null when the system itself triggered (e.g. TTL-driven revocation).

payload.description: Per-type payload. Unknown types — or unknown fields within a known type — MUST be forwarded by SDKs as opaque data so old clients against new servers continue to receive events. See $defs for the canonical shape of each type.

revoker_id.description: Null when the system auto-revoked on TTL.
